## Summary

Downloads were permanently blocked ("temporarily unavailable") for all evaluations persisted before the write-time sanitizer (commit `8c9c5cf0`). Root cause: the `downloadParityGate` correctly rejects contaminated text ("would because", "safe injection sites", double modals), but no read-time cleanup existed — so stored artifacts with those patterns could NEVER be downloaded. This adds `sanitizeResultForDownload()` applied to both the download route and the webpage eval page before validation/rendering.

## Scope

- `lib/evaluation/downloadReadTimeSanitizer.ts` — new read-time sanitizer module
- `app/api/reports/[jobId]/download/route.ts` — sanitize before parity gate
- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — sanitize before webpage rendering
- `__tests__/lib/evaluation/downloadReadTimeSanitizer.test.ts` — 9 unit tests
- `__tests__/lib/evaluation/downloadMistakeProofing.test.ts` — 9 integration/mistake-proofing tests

Out of scope: write-time sanitizer changes, parity gate logic changes, template rendering changes.

## Tests Updated

- `downloadReadTimeSanitizer.test.ts`: 9 tests covering all malformed pattern replacements, graceful handling of missing fields, no corruption of clean data
- `downloadMistakeProofing.test.ts`: 9 tests covering real production contamination samples, multi-field contamination, template completeness preservation, and idempotency

## Visual Evidence

N/A — no UI layout changes; sanitization is text-level cleanup of stored data.

N/A — non-UI change.

N/A — non-UI change.

N/A — no CI/infra behavior changes.

## Rollback Plan

Revert this commit. Downloads will revert to being blocked for pre-sanitizer artifacts. No data loss risk.

N/A — no workflow changes.

N/A — no schema changes.

N/A — no backfill. Read-time sanitization handles legacy data at access time.

N/A — no access-control changes.

N/A — no schema changes.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

Input: stored `evaluation_result_v2` artifacts loaded from Supabase. No new input sources. Authorization unchanged (user must own the job).

## Internal Process Leakage

Confirmed: no internal process details exposed. Sanitizer replaces contaminated fragments with clean prose equivalents.

## Input → Action → Output

User clicks Download → API loads stored artifact → `sanitizeResultForDownload()` cleans text → parity gate validates → canonical template renders → file delivered.

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

N/A — no new metrics exposed.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

None. This adds ~0.5ms of regex processing per download request.

## Latency Impact

Negligible — regex sanitization on a single JSON object adds <1ms. No network calls.

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: no

Pass selection (check exactly one):

- [ ] Pass 1
- [x] Pass 2
- [ ] Pass 3

- [ ] Sequential phase-gate doctrine preserved (parallelism only within safe sub-workloads).
- [ ] Phase 0 remains first and is proven before downstream processing.
- [ ] Phase 2 remains blocked on accepted_story_ledger_v1 (Review Gate authority).
- [ ] Phase 3 remains blocked on pass12_handoff_v1 and is sole owner of Pass 3B synthesis.
- [ ] Deterministic quality gates run after Pass 3B and before completion.
- [ ] WAVE remains post-evaluation (after evaluation_result_v2) and non-fatal to base evaluation.

One-line doctrine: The pipeline is sequential at the phase/gate level and parallel only inside safe sub-workloads.

## Contract Integrity

The read-time sanitizer uses the SAME pattern set as the write-time sanitizer (`persistEvaluationResultV2.ts`) and the parity gate (`downloadParityGate.ts`). All three are now aligned on the same contamination vocabulary.

## Behavioral Quality

This PR is not reducing intelligence. It's unblocking delivery of already-computed evaluation content.

## Quality Gate / Anomalies

QG_none — No quality gate or anomaly impact. Sanitization is deterministic regex replacement.

## Risk

Low risk. Sanitizer only replaces known-bad patterns with clean equivalents. Idempotent. Falls back to original text if sanitization would produce empty string.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

None. Patterns are identical to the already-deployed write-time sanitizer.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: This PR does not modify the evaluation pipeline. It only sanitizes already-persisted artifacts at read time for the download/display path.
